Description: Chat history is a single global ChatMessage table with no user/session scoping (route.ts:67-74, 124-127). Every caller reads and writes the same conversation, so different users see and influence each other's chat history.
Root Cause Hypothesis: ChatMessage model (schema.prisma:387-393) has no userId/sessionId column; the route queries the whole table.

Description: No length/size validation on message or context (route.ts:54-58). Unbounded strings are persisted to SQLite and forwarded to the LLM, enabling cost/DoS abuse and oversized token requests.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Validation only checks presence and type, not bounds.

Description: When Z.ai returns no content, the fallback error string is saved into ChatMessage as a genuine assistant turn (route.ts:109-120), polluting future conversation history that gets re-injected as context.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fallback assignment happens before the unconditional create() with no success check.

Description: GET returns the OLDEST 50 messages (orderBy asc + take 50) (route.ts:139-142), so once the table exceeds 50 rows the most recent messages are never returned by this endpoint — inconsistent with POST which returns newest 20 desc (route.ts:124-127).
Root Cause Hypothesis: Ascending order combined with take:50 caps at the start of the table instead of the tail.

Description: DELETE deletes the entire ChatMessage table unconditionally with no auth and no scoping (route.ts:146-148). Any anonymous request destroys all users' chat history.
Root Cause Hypothesis: deleteMany() called with no where clause and no authentication guard.

Description: topK from query string is not validated (rag/route.ts:64,115). Non-numeric values yield NaN and slice(0,NaN) returns an empty result set even when matches exist; negative values produce an unintended slice.
Root Cause Hypothesis: parseInt result passed directly to Array.slice without NaN/range guard.

Description: GET has no try/catch (rag/route.ts:61-127); a DB failure surfaces as an unhandled rejection rather than a controlled error response.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only POST-style error handling exists for the AI path; the retrieval path is unguarded.

Description: POST /api/rag has no top-level try/catch (rag/route.ts:130-251). A malformed JSON body (req.json() at 131), a DB write failure, or a non-balance API error throws an unhandled rejection returning a raw 500 with no controlled body.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Error handling only wraps the LLM call (212-222); request parsing and persistence are unguarded.

Description: In the 503 insufficient-balance branch, `sources: top` returns full internal doc objects (id, tokens) (rag/route.ts:218) whereas the success path maps to {ref,book,text,score} (rag/route.ts:244-249) — inconsistent/over-exposed shape including the raw token arrays.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Different serialization in the error branch vs success branch.

Description: topK from the body is never validated as a number (rag/route.ts:132); a string topK flows into Array.slice (165) producing undefined behaviour for non-numeric inputs.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Default value supplied but no type coercion/guard.

Description: chapterNum is validated as truthy (summarize/route.ts:13), so a legitimate chapter numbered 0 is rejected with 400; also no string->int coercion, so a JSON string chapterNum reaches a Prisma Int field (route.ts:21) risking a type error or silent miss.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Presence check uses falsiness instead of `=== undefined`/Number.isInteger, and trusts client JSON type.

Description: No top-level try/catch (summarize/route.ts:9-130). req.json() on a malformed body, a DB error, or a non-balance API error throws an unhandled rejection -> raw 500 with no controlled error body.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only the LLM call is wrapped (88-105); parsing and persistence are unguarded.

Description: The fallback no-content summary string is unconditionally written to Chapter.summary (route.ts:107,110-113), overwriting any previously valid editorial/AI summary with an error placeholder when the LLM returns empty.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Persist happens regardless of whether real content was produced.

Description: Confidence/credibility math can be undermined: Evidence.confidence = credibility*0.7 and blindspot = credibility<0.6 (scrape/route.ts:223,227), but scoreCredibility floors unknown domains at 0.55 and adds keyword bonus, so low-quality unknown sources get auto-corroborated Evidence and a passing ReviewRecord with no human review.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auto-corroboration writes review/audit records as if vetted, conflating heuristic scoring with editorial approval.

Description: `found: searchResults.length` (scrape/route.ts:260) reports the full result count while only Math.min(num,8) results are ever processed/saved (scrape/route.ts:129), so found can exceed the number actually considered — misleading metric.
Root Cause Hypothesis: found derived from the raw array length rather than topResults.

Description: num is not validated (scrape/route.ts:91); negative or non-numeric num flows into Math.min and slice, producing empty or unexpected processing sets silently.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Default supplied but no numeric/range guard.

Description: The 'verses needing evidence' query is broken: the NOT/id-in filter maps evidence rows to '' for every element — `(await db.evidence.findMany({select:{scriptureRef:true}})).map(() => '')` (auto-grow/route.ts:93) produces an array of empty strings, so the exclusion is meaningless. The result (versesNeedingEvidence) is then never used; the code falls through to a hardcoded `text contains 'Watchers'` query (auto-grow/route.ts:101-107). The advertised 'verses lacking evidence' behaviour does not happen.
Root Cause Hypothesis: map callback ignores the row and returns '' (acknowledged by the inline comment at 100); dead query left in place.

Description: ReviewRecord is linked via a re-query: itemId comes from db.evidence.findFirst({where:{sourceId}, orderBy createdAt desc}) (auto-grow/route.ts:229). On reruns or when multiple evidence rows share a sourceId, this can attach the ReviewRecord to the wrong evidence; the just-created evidence id from line 211 is discarded instead of being captured.
Root Cause Hypothesis: db.evidence.create result is not assigned to a variable, forcing a fragile lookup.

Description: No request validation or auth on a maxDuration 300s endpoint that performs external network fetches and bulk DB writes (auto-grow/route.ts:26-30). mode and limit are unvalidated; a large limit or repeated calls can drive cost/load.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Endpoint trusts client input and is publicly reachable.

Description: No top-level try/catch around req.json() (auto-grow/route.ts:27); a malformed body throws an unhandled rejection before the GrowResult scaffolding is built.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Body parse is outside any guard.

Description: GET status calls checkApiHealth (auto-grow/route.ts:257) which POSTs a real chat/completions request to Z.ai on every status poll (zai-api.ts:223-236), incurring a billable API call as a side effect of a read-only status endpoint.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Health probe is an active LLM call rather than a lightweight/cached check.

Description: A real Z.ai API key literal is hardcoded as the default for API_KEY (zai-api.ts:4: `process.env.ZAI_API_KEY || '<redacted-rotate-me>'`). This secret is committed to the repo and used by webSearch, pageReader, and checkApiHealth whenever the env var is unset, exposing the credential and risking unauthorized billing.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Convenience default left in source; should be required env with no fallback.
Verification: Removed hardcoded Z.ai key from src/lib/zai-api.ts; env-only + graceful fallback. Gate green. (Leaked key still needs owner rotation.)

Description: request_id is `ws-${Date.now()}`.slice(0,30) (zai-api.ts:46); under concurrent same-millisecond calls request_ids collide, which can confuse server-side request correlation/idempotency.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Timestamp-only id with no randomness/counter.

Description: When data.data / data.results is an array it is returned verbatim (zai-api.ts:109-111) without coercion to the SearchResult shape, so downstream consumers (scrape/auto-grow expecting r.url, r.snippet) may receive missing fields and create Source rows with title=url and empty snippet.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Pass-through return assumes upstream already matches the interface.

Description: directFetchPage (zai-api.ts:180-207) fetches any caller-supplied URL with no allowlist/scheme/host restriction. Because scrape (scrape/route.ts:134) and auto-grow (auto-grow/route.ts:173) feed it URLs that ultimately originate from external/AI search results, an attacker who can influence the search results (or a malicious scriptureRef-driven query) could induce server-side requests to internal addresses (SSRF).
Root Cause Hypothesis: No URL validation before server-side fetch; trusts upstream search output.

Description: error.code is compared against string literals '1113'/'1211' (zai-api.ts:239,242); if the API returns a numeric code these comparisons silently fail and fall through to the generic branch, mislabeling balance/model errors.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Assumes code is a string without normalizing the type.

Description: chapter=0 is mishandled: src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:31 uses `chapterNum ? parseInt(...) : ...` and the result feeds a truthiness check at line 32 (`if (!targetChapter)`). parseInt('0',10)===0 is falsy, so chapter '0' falls back to the first chapter rather than querying chapter 0.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Falsy-zero handling: numeric 0 treated the same as missing/invalid because of `!targetChapter` and the `chapterNum ?` guard.

Description: Comment at src/app/api/fts/route.ts:16-17 says 'use OR for multi-word' but the implementation joins quoted tokens with a space, which is implicit AND in FTS5. Multi-word searches therefore require ALL terms, contradicting documented intent.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing ' OR '.join(...) — tokens are space-joined instead of OR-joined.

Description: rank is computed as `-i` (negative array index) at src/app/api/fts/route.ts:35, not a real bm25 relevance score. The comment acknowledges bm25 'needs to be in the same query' but it was never added, so results are unranked / ordered only by SQLite's default row order.
Root Cause Hypothesis: bm25() ranking was deferred and replaced with a positional placeholder; ORDER BY rank is absent in the SQL.

Description: limit from `parseInt(searchParams.get('limit') || '20', 10)` at src/app/api/fts/route.ts:10 is not validated; a non-numeric value yields NaN and a negative value is passed directly into SQL LIMIT (only the verse query; sources/evidence are Math.min-clamped but a negative limit still flows through as Math.min(neg,10)=neg).
Root Cause Hypothesis: No clamping/validation of the parsed limit before binding into the raw query.

Description: The theme-list branch (src/app/api/themes/route.ts:12-17) eagerly includes every verseLink (and each verse's book) solely to compute verseCount = verseLinks.length (line 25), instead of using Prisma _count. This loads the entire join graph for all themes on every list request.
Root Cause Hypothesis: verseCount derived in JS from a full include rather than a relation _count aggregate; N+ row hydration overhead.

Description: Verse selection is limited to the first 200 rows via `take: 200` at src/app/api/daily-insight/route.ts:19, so any verse beyond index 199 can never be chosen as the daily insight regardless of dayOfYear.
Root Cause Hypothesis: take: 200 was likely a guard against loading the whole corpus, but it silently caps the rotation pool.

Description: Find-then-create on date is not atomic (src/app/api/daily-insight/route.ts:12 findUnique then :52 create). Two concurrent first-of-day requests can both pass the existence check and the second create will violate the date @unique constraint, throwing an unhandled 500.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No upsert / transaction; check-then-act race on the unique date key.

Description: Type error at src/app/api/flashcards/route.ts:86/98: `const created = []` infers type `never[]`, so `created.push(card)` at line 98 (card being a Prisma Flashcard) is a TypeScript error (`Argument of type 'Flashcard' is not assignable to parameter of type 'never'`). Will fail tsc/build with strict typing.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Array literal initialized without an explicit element type, so TS infers never[]; needs `const created: Flashcard[] = []` or typed accumulation.

Description: No request-body validation at src/app/api/flashcards/route.ts:28-29: bookSlug/chapterNum are not checked before use. A missing bookSlug passes undefined into findUnique({ where: { slug: undefined } }) and chapterNum string/number coercion is not enforced against the Int schema.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Body destructured and used directly without presence/type guards.

Description: No authentication or rate limiting on a paid AI + write endpoint (src/app/api/flashcards/route.ts:27). Any caller can incur Z.ai cost and create rows.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Endpoint ships without auth middleware or throttling.

Description: Deleting a non-existent id at src/app/api/flashcards/route.ts:150 calls db.flashcard.delete without catching Prisma's RecordNotFound (P2025); the error is unhandled and surfaces as a 500 instead of a 404.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No existence check or try/catch around delete; relies on caller supplying a valid id.

Description: Updating a non-existent id at src/app/api/notes/route.ts:48 calls db.note.update without catching Prisma's P2025 RecordNotFound, surfacing as a 500 rather than a 404.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No existence check or try/catch; consistent with the delete handlers lacking not-found handling.

Description: Deleting a non-existent id at src/app/api/notes/route.ts:63 calls db.note.delete without catching Prisma P2025, surfacing as a 500 instead of 404.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No existence check or try/catch around the delete.

Description: No authentication on any evidence endpoint; private-research-layer evidence is world-readable via GET /api/evidence (route.ts:7-36)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auth was never implemented; the 3-layer architecture assumes a network boundary that does not exist in code

Description: ref and sourceId branches have no take limit unlike the default branch (route.ts:13,22 vs 33), so a hot scriptureRef returns an unbounded result set
Root Cause Hypothesis: take:200 was only added to the fallback branch; the filtered branches were assumed to be naturally small

Description: confidence accepted with no range validation; schema comment says 0..1 (route.ts:53; schema.prisma:198) but any number is persisted
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only a typeof check was added; bounds clamping was never implemented

Description: PATCH on a nonexistent id throws an unhandled Prisma error → generic 500 instead of 404 (route.ts:49)
Root Cause Hypothesis: No try/catch around db.evidence.update; missing-record case not handled

Description: No authentication — any caller can edit any evidence record (route.ts:39)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auth layer never built

Description: DELETE writes no AuditLog entry, so destructive removals are untraceable even though AuditLog enumerates a 'delete' action (route.ts:60-66; schema.prisma:247)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Audit logging was only wired into review/publish POST handlers, not into evidence DELETE
Verification: Evidence DELETE now writes an AuditLog 'delete' entry + 404 on missing id. Gate green.

Description: Deleting evidence leaves orphaned ReviewRecord and AuditLog rows (itemId is an unconstrained string) and leaves any published PublicArticle live (route.ts:64)
Root Cause Hypothesis: FK-by-id-string design has no referential integrity; publish path is one-way with no unpublish-on-delete

Description: No authentication on DELETE — anyone can hard-delete research records (route.ts:60)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auth layer never built

Description: Comment says 'Get the latest ReviewRecord per item' but the query has no per-item dedupe/groupBy, so all versions are returned (route.ts:25-30)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Versioning was added (version field, newVersion in POST) but the GET was never updated to select max(version) per item

Description: itemType 'source' is declared valid (VALID_ITEM_TYPES line 8) but has no hydration branch, so source review records never appear in the queue (route.ts:36-48,60)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Source review was scoped into the type list but the hydration switch was never extended

Description: counts omits 'archived' though it is a valid state (route.ts:62-69)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Counts object hand-written and archived case forgotten

Description: No auth — review queue and full audit log are world-readable (route.ts:10)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auth layer never built

Description: No state-machine transition guard: stateMap[action] is applied unconditionally, so any item can be approved from any state — including straight from 'draft' or re-approving a 'rejected' item (route.ts:98,142-167). The editorial gate can be bypassed.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only an action→state map was implemented; no current-state precondition matrix was ever added
Verification: Added editorial state-transition guard to review POST (409 on illegal transition). Verified logic + gate green.

Description: No authorization: reviewer and reviewerRole come from the request body with defaults, so any caller can self-assign 'publisher'/'reviewer' and approve content (route.ts:76). There is zero authentication on the editorial gate.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Roles were modeled in the schema (reviewerRole/actorRole) but never enforced; no auth middleware exists

Description: Schema drift: request-revision writes 'needs-revision' to evidence.reviewState but the Evidence.reviewState enum comment (schema.prisma:204) does not include 'needs-revision' (review route VALID_STATES does, line 7).
Root Cause Hypothesis: needs-revision added to the review workflow after the evidence model comment was written; never reconciled
Verification: Reconciled Evidence.reviewState schema comment to include 'needs-revision'. prisma validate OK.

Description: itemType 'source' is accepted by VALID_ITEM_TYPES but has no item-update branch and Source has no reviewState field, so a ReviewRecord+AuditLog are created while the source itself is never transitioned (route.ts:86,142-167; schema.prisma:127-149)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Source review was declared but never implemented on the model or the update switch

Description: Transitioning an approved item to rejected/archived/needs-revision resets publishedAt to null (route.ts:148,156,164), silently clearing the approval timestamp on de-approval
Root Cause Hypothesis: publishedAt is set inline by a ternary keyed only on newState=='approved' with no else-preserve logic

Description: Checklist auto-compute only covers itemType=='evidence' (route.ts:103); public-article and topic-page transitions store a null checklist unless the caller supplies one
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only the evidence checklist was implemented

Description: No authentication on the publish endpoint; actor/actorRole are hardcoded 'publisher' (route.ts:143-144) so any anonymous caller can push approved content live. Combined with F-ED-005's self-elevation, the whole approve→publish gate is bypassable.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auth never implemented; publisher identity is faked as a constant

Description: corroborationScore and confidenceScore are hardcoded to 0.5 on create with comment 'computed later' and are never updated on re-publish (route.ts:119-120). The public 'how we vet' scores are fabricated, not derived from sources×perspectives as the schema comment promises (schema.prisma:293).
Root Cause Hypothesis: Score computation was deferred and never built; placeholders shipped

Description: sourcesCount hardcoded to 1 (route.ts:121) regardless of how many independent sources corroborate the claim, undermining the corroboration model the public site advertises
Root Cause Hypothesis: Single-evidence publish path assumed one source; multi-source aggregation never implemented

Description: On the update branch, perspectivesCount (and counts generally) are not recomputed (route.ts:94-106 vs create at 108-124), so counts drift on re-publish
Root Cause Hypothesis: Create and update data objects were written separately; update omits the count fields

Description: One-way publish with no unpublish: if an evidence/topic-page is later rejected or archived via review, its already-published PublicArticle stays live with reviewState='approved' (route.ts hardcodes reviewState:'approved' on every publish; nothing reverts it)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Publish path is explicitly one-way ('private → public'); no retraction handler exists

Description: Public article list surfaces corroborationScore/confidenceScore that are hardcoded 0.5 placeholders set by the publish endpoint (route.ts:44-45 select; originate from publish/route.ts:119-120). Readers see fabricated trust signals.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Inherited from F-ED-006's unimplemented score computation

Description: The topic page itself is served regardless of its reviewState; only its articles are gated to 'approved' (route.ts:56-66). Anyone with the slug can read the full bodyMarkdown of a draft/rejected/archived topic page, leaking unvetted content past the editorial gate.
Root Cause Hypothesis: The reviewState='approved' filter was applied to the nested publicArticles include but not to the top-level findUnique on TopicPage
Verification: Public topic page gated on reviewState='approved' (findFirst). Verified at runtime: unapproved slug -> HTTP 404, approved -> 200.

Description: reviewStates, credibilityTiers and reviewRubric are hardcoded in the public route (route.ts:108-131) duplicating the canonical lists in review/route.ts VALID_STATES (line 7) and the schema comments (schema.prisma:133,204,232); they can silently drift
Root Cause Hypothesis: No single source of truth for taxonomy constants; copied into each consumer

Description: GET /api/export dumps the entire private database (sources, evidence, notes, chat history) with zero authentication (route.ts:7-48). Full unauthenticated data exfiltration.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Export was built as an internal backup tool assuming a trusted/private deployment; no auth gate added

Description: Loads and serializes the whole DB (including Source.rawHtml/content) in a single in-memory response with no streaming or pagination (route.ts:8-48)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Simple all-at-once Promise.all backup; scale not considered

Description: Health endpoint (which surfaces auth status and account balance per the file header) has no authentication (route.ts:8-15), exposing provider account info to anyone
Root Cause Hypothesis: Health route treated as internal/ops-only without an auth gate

Description: The computed checklist is purely informational — it is stored on the ReviewRecord but never gates the approve action (route.ts:101-119 vs 129-167). An evidence item that fails every rubric criterion (no source, self-published tier, no perspectives) can still be approved and published.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Checklist computation and the state transition were built independently; no enforcement step ties failing criteria to a blocked transition

Description: noFabrication and authorityNotOverstated are hardcoded true (route.ts:114,116) so two of the seven rubric items are always 'passing' regardless of reality, weakening the checklist's signal
Root Cause Hypothesis: These two are intended to be human-confirmed but there is no mechanism to capture that confirmation; placeholders ship as true

Description: A caller-supplied checklist is stored verbatim with no validation (route.ts:102,136), so a client can fabricate an all-passing checklist
Root Cause Hypothesis: checklist defaults to the request body value before the evidence-only compute branch

Description: Active tab is not reflected in the URL or persisted; refresh always returns to 'study' (page.tsx:42). The header buttons are not marked with aria-current / role='tab' / aria-selected, so screen readers cannot announce which view is active.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Tab is plain useState with no router sync and no ARIA tab semantics on the custom buttons.

Description: runSearch() has no try/catch around fetch/res.json() (page.tsx:95-101); a network failure or non-200 response throws an unhandled promise rejection and shows no error toast/message to the user.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Only a try/finally is used (finally resets `searching`), with no catch and no error state — there is no useToast usage anywhere in page.tsx despite the Toaster being mounted in layout.

Description: Evidence result count is displayed (searchResults.evidence?.length, page.tsx:312) but evidence records are never rendered in the results list, only verses and sources are.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Incomplete results UI — evidence rendering block was never added.

Description: Trailing '...' is appended unconditionally to the verse snippet regardless of length (page.tsx:276), so short verses display a misleading ellipsis.
Root Cause Hypothesis: `slice(0,80)` plus hard-coded '...' without a length check.

Description: The clear (X) button on the indicator bar has no aria-label/title (page.tsx:277-286); screen-reader users get an unlabeled button.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Icon-only button without accessible name.

Description: exportBackup() (page.tsx:104-114) has no try/catch and no loading/disabled state; an export failure throws silently with no user feedback.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fire-and-forget async with no error handling or toast.

Description: User feedback for grow/scrape/publish relies on native window.alert/confirm (page.tsx:118,131,132,135) despite a shadcn Toaster being mounted in layout.tsx:183; inconsistent and blocking UX.
Root Cause Hypothesis: useToast/Toaster infrastructure exists but is never wired into page.tsx; alerts used as a shortcut.

Description: Health check result is never surfaced as a visible status indicator — it only conditionally disables the (mobile-hidden) Grow button. On mobile the entire health signal is invisible. The `model` field is fetched but never shown (page.tsx:54).
Root Cause Hypothesis: Minimal use of health data; no dedicated status UI.

Description: page.tsx reimplements its own mobile detection (inline useEffect at page.tsx:57-66) instead of using the existing useIsMobile hook (src/hooks/use-mobile.ts), duplicating logic with a different start value (false vs undefined) and an undebounced resize handler.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Shared hook not adopted; ad-hoc inline implementation.

Description: Mobile bottom-nav buttons (page.tsx:370-396) have no aria-label or aria-current; active panel is conveyed only by color, hurting screen-reader and keyboard users.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Custom buttons without ARIA tab semantics.

Description: All three scripture fetches swallow errors with empty catch blocks (scripture-reader.tsx:83, 98-99, 131) and there is no error UI; an API failure is indistinguishable from an empty chapter ('No verses found.'), and the initial books fetch failing leaves the reader permanently blank with no retry.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Defensive empty catches with no error state variable or toast.

Description: Verse rows are clickable <div>s (scripture-reader.tsx:308-318) with onClick but no role='button', tabIndex, or keydown handler — verse selection is impossible via keyboard.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Div-as-button anti-pattern without ARIA/keyboard support.

Description: copyVerse (scripture-reader.tsx:166-168) provides no success/failure feedback and no clipboard fallback; on rejection the promise is unhandled.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fire-and-forget clipboard write.

Description: The per-verse Copy button only becomes visible on mouse hover via opacity (scripture-reader.tsx:341-344), making it unreachable for keyboard and touch users.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Hover-only affordance with no focus-within reveal.

Description: Initial history fetch (chat-panel.tsx:31-40) swallows errors with .catch(()=>{}) and shows no indication if history failed to load.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Silent catch with no error state for the GET path (only POST sets error).

Description: The Clear-history Trash button (chat-panel.tsx:112-119) and Send button (chat-panel.tsx:225-232) are icon-only with no aria-label/title.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Icon buttons missing accessible names.

Description: Initial evidence/cross-ref Promise.all (synergy-view.tsx:67-77) has no .catch — a failed fetch rejects unhandled and leaves loading stuck false only via .finally; no error UI.
Root Cause Hypothesis: .finally clears loading but there's no catch/error state.

Description: updateAlignment PATCH (synergy-view.tsx:125-134) optimistically updates UI with no error handling/rollback; a failed PATCH silently diverges UI from server.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No await error check, no rollback.

Description: The delete (Trash2) icon button (synergy-view.tsx:307) has no aria-label.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Icon-only button without accessible name.

Description: The graph help text says 'Hover or click to inspect' (synergy-view.tsx:433) but no node has any hover or click handler — the affordance described does not exist.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Copy describes intended/future interactivity never implemented.

Description: The SVG knowledge graph has no role/aria-label/title/desc (synergy-view.tsx:437) and relies on absolute pixel layout; it is inaccessible to screen readers and can overflow with many nodes.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Decorative-style SVG without accessibility annotations.

Description: Timeline scripture composition dates are hardcoded to exactly three books (synergy-view.tsx:597-601), so the empty-state 'No dated items yet.' is effectively unreachable and the timeline always shows the same 3 entries regardless of the actual book set.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Placeholder hardcoded data rather than deriving dates from book metadata.

Description: ThemeExplorer fetch (theme-explorer.tsx:29-33) has no .catch; a failure only clears loading via .finally, leaving a permanently blank list with no error or empty-state message.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing catch and missing empty state (themes.length===0 not handled).

Description: Theme cards (theme-explorer.tsx:86-94) are clickable Cards with onClick but no role='button'/tabIndex/keydown — selecting a theme is impossible by keyboard.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Clickable Card without interactive semantics.

Description: Daily insight fetch (study-tools.tsx:40-42) has no .catch and no error/timeout handling; on failure `daily` stays null and the loading spinner (line 149-153) spins indefinitely with no recovery.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Promise chain with no catch and loading inferred from null data rather than an explicit state.

Description: generateSummary (study-tools.tsx:63-77) uses try/finally with no catch; a failed POST throws and is swallowed (finally resets loading), and on a malformed success `setSummary(d.summary)` can set undefined with no error feedback.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No catch branch and no response.ok check.

Description: reviewCard (study-tools.tsx:99-112) issues the PATCH with no try/catch; a failed grade rejects unhandled while the UI still advances and reloads, risking UI/server divergence.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No error handling around the await fetch.

Description: The flashcard flip surface (study-tools.tsx:214-217) is a clickable div with no role='button'/tabIndex/keyboard handler, so flipping is mouse/touch-only.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Clickable div without interactive semantics.

Description: JSON.parse(g.perspectiveNotes) is called inline during render (study-tools.tsx:281) without a try/catch; any malformed perspectiveNotes value throws and crashes the Glossary render (and there is no React error boundary configured in layout).
Root Cause Hypothesis: Unguarded JSON.parse in JSX render path.

Description: Glossary fetch (study-tools.tsx:45-47) has no catch and no loading/empty state; failure or empty data yields a silent blank panel.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing loading/empty/error handling.

Description: ReviewDashboard.load() (review-dashboard.tsx:77-85) awaits fetch with no try/catch; any failure throws, leaving loading=true so the spinner persists indefinitely with no error UI.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No error handling around the load fetch; loading only set false on the happy path.

Description: checklist is parsed via JSON.parse(selected.checklist) at render time (review-dashboard.tsx:136) with no try/catch; a malformed checklist string throws and breaks the detail pane.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Unguarded JSON.parse in render path.

Description: act() (review-dashboard.tsx:91-113) uses try/finally with no catch and no res.ok check; a failed review action is silently swallowed (queue reloads, selection cleared) so the editor gets no confirmation or error.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No error branch on the review POST.

Description: Reviewer text input (review-dashboard.tsx:345-350) has no associated <label>/htmlFor and no validation; an empty reviewer can be submitted.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Bare input with sibling span instead of a proper label, no required check.

Description: Only the includeDrafts fetch has a .catch (public-site.tsx:55); the published-topics and transparency fetches in the same Promise.all have no catch, so either failing rejects unhandled (loading is cleared only by .finally) with a blank UI.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Inconsistent error handling across the three parallel fetches.

Description: Topic cards (public-site.tsx:121-123) are clickable Cards with onClick but no role='button'/tabIndex/keydown — not keyboard accessible.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Clickable Card without interactive semantics.

Description: viewTopic (public-site.tsx:69-72) has no error handling and unconditionally setSelected(d.topicPage); a failed fetch or missing topicPage sets selected to undefined and silently falls back to the transparency/spinner view with no feedback.
Root Cause Hypothesis: No catch and no guard on the response shape.

Description: robots.ts disallows /study and /review and OG/canonical point at public marketing routes (/topics,/articles,/glossary,/scripture), but the actual application is a single-page client app rooted at '/' with in-page tabs (page.tsx); those crawlable public routes are referenced by robots/sitemap yet are not represented among the files in scope, risking sitemap entries that 404 if those routes aren't implemented.
Root Cause Hypothesis: SEO config anticipates a multi-route public site while the in-scope UI is a one-page tabbed app; route existence not verifiable from these files.

Description: The toast system is mounted globally (layout.tsx:183) but never used by any in-scope feature; all user feedback for async actions uses blocking window.alert/confirm (page.tsx, synergy-view.tsx, review-dashboard.tsx) or silent catches, so errors across search/export/summary/daily/themes/review/public are largely invisible despite available non-blocking toast infrastructure.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Toaster scaffolding from the shadcn template was never wired into feature error handling.

Description: useToast's useEffect dependency array is [state] (use-toast.ts:185), causing the listener to be removed/re-added on every state change rather than once on mount.
Root Cause Hypothesis: Incorrect dependency array (should be [] ) carried over from the template.
